title: Leibnitzia nepalensis
intro: Leibnitzia nepalensis, also known as the Nepal leibnitzia or Nepal tea, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Leibnitzia nepalensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leibnitzia nepalensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Leibnitzia nepalensis is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and seedheads. The genus Leibnitzia is a small one, native to the Himalayas, and is often separated from other related genera based on its unique floral features. Leibnitzia nepalensis is often placed in the tribe Inuleae, a group of plants that share similar characteristics with the daisy family. The classification of Leibnitzia nepalensis is subject to ongoing research and revision, but Asteraceae remains the most widely accepted classification for this plant.

Its roots and leaves are used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold. Leibnitzia nepalensis is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal uses.
